observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Treasurer complete the investigation to validate the existence of the unverified buildings and other land improvements; otherwise, proceed with the derecognition process prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
direct the Municipal Treasurer to request authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to authorize the Local Chief Executive to transfer the completed projects of prior years to the respective beneficiaries, duly supported by Property Transfer Receipt for those to be transferred to other levels of government, and Deed of Donation for private organizations; and
direct the Municipal Accountant to draw the Journal Entry Vouchers for the dropping of the affected PPE accounts from the books upon receipt of the SB authorization, Property Transfer Receipt, and Deed of Donation.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
Municipal Treasurer immediately prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and submit the same to the Audit Team to facilitate the request for inspection to COA Regional Office No. XI;
Disposal Committee undertake the immediate disposal procedures of unserviceable properties within the 1st semester of 2025 to avoid possible loss due to further deterioration and devaluation of said properties; and
Municipal Accountant draw the Journal Entry Vouchers for the dropping of the affected PPE accounts from the books upon receipt of the approved IIRUP following the disposal made of the unserviceable properties.

We reiterated the following recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the certified list of settlement of the disallowances together with the supporting documents not later than 1st semester of 2025 for CY 2024 settlements, and to regularly submit, either on a quarterly/monthly basis, copies of journal entry vouchers and certified payrolls of settlements made by the persons liable to support issuance of subsequent Notice of Settlement of Suspensions/Disallowances/Charges; and
direct the Municipal Accountant, through the Municipal Administrator’s Office, to issue demand letters to the persons liable to enforce collection and recover the receivables on disallowances.
We further recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipality file claims against the estate of the deceased persons liable or enforce collection against the other persons named liable for the disallowance.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant require:
all drivers to regularly prepare and accurately accomplish Daily Trip Tickets and Monthly Report of Official Travels, using the prescribed form under COA Circular No. 77-61, for submission to the Municipal Accounting Office; and
implementing offices prepare the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption using the prescribed form under COA Circular No. 77-61, and submit regularly to COA for verification purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Accountant, Supply/Property Division/Unit, and the Disposal Committee to implement the following course of action:

The Supply/Property Division/Unit to immediately prepare an updated and duly accomplished Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and submit the same to the Audit Team to facilitate the request for inspection to COA-Region Office No. XI.

The Disposal Committee to undertake the immediate disposal procedures of unserviceable properties within the 1st semester of 2024 to avoid possible loss due to further deterioration and devaluation of said properties.

The Supply/Property Division/ Unit submits to the Municipal Accountant the original copy of the approved IIRUP following the disposal made, who shall then validate the supporting documents, and prepare the necessary entries to derecognize the unserviceable PPEs from the books of accounts. Furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the said JEV, including its supporting documents for its reference.

The Supply/Property Division/ Unit shall carry out the necessary procedures to complete the transfer of the PPE items to the recipient government agencies within the 1st quarter of 2024, such as Sangguniang Bayan resolution granting the transfer, Property Transfer Report, and Acknowledgment Receipt and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant.

Upon receipt of the complete supporting documents, the Municipal Accountant shall immediately draw the necessary journal entries to derecognize the following items in the books of accounts and shall submit the same to the Audit Team for reference.



The Supply/Property Division/ Unit shall conduct an investigation, in coordination with the Municipal Engineering Office for the Buildings and Infrastructure Assets, to validate the existence of the P13 million unverified or unlocated properties within the 1st semester of 2024.

Results of the aforementioned investigation shall be summarized in a formal report indicating the actions to be taken in the event that some properties are not actually located despite exhaustive efforts made for the Audit Team’s review and further action.

observations.recommendation:
The policy for the operation of the Heavy Equipment Rental should be revisited. See to it that the objectives for which this economic enterprise was ventured shall be attained.  Utilization of the heavy equipment by clientele who cannot afford to pay rental as in the case of barangay, may be recognized with a corresponding subsidy account to be incorporated in the financial plan of the economic enterprise. 



Otherwise, the Municipality may cease the operation of the Heavy Equipment Rental as economic enterprise and turn it over as a regular operation of the municipality under the general fund proper.
observations.reason:
Kapalong Rev. Revenue Code 2018-2023 (Mun. Ordinance No. 1036 dated 09.18.17) the EEDMO supervises the Motorpool & Heavy Equipment Operation.

However, utilization by barangays who cannot afford to pay rental was not recorded as subsidy as such was not duly stipulated in the Kapalong Rev. Revenue Code 2018-2023. 

During CY 2022 exit conference, management informed the audit team of measures undertaken by them which results/ improvement in the operations will be seen in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following courses of action:
Require the Municipal Accountant to draw appropriate JEV entries to recognize the transferred assets and its corresponding depreciation expense, if applicable, so that balances of the affected asset accounts are accurately presented in the financial statements as at December 31, 2022.
Instruct the Municipal Administrator to coordinate with all offices and conduct re-orientation on the proper procedure and action to be done upon receipt of any properties/inventories that were delivered directly to them by the suppliers/donors.
Communicate with DENR-EMB and PAGRO for further necessary actions in order to immediately settle the lacking documents in the recording of the donated / transferred assets in the books of Municipal Government.

We recommended that management re-evaluate those projects tagged as not implemented to determine whether these projects could be completed during the year, direct the preparation of request for re-alignment to the Municipal Development Council if warranted.
We recommended that the BAC together with the implementing offices conduct early procurement activities so that project implementation will be completed within the expected timelines.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineering Office to fast-track the completion of partially completed projects.
We recommended that the implementing offices submit to the Municipal Accountant copies of Certificate of Completion and Project Acceptance so that the return of the unutilized funds and timely liquidation thereof will be submitted to source agencies in accordance with the memorandum of agreement.
For projects 4 and 5, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant cause the deduction of liquidated damages from the contractor’s final billing for the delays incurred in project completion.
We also recommended that the Management ensure that corrective works are immediately undertaken to prevent further damage to the newly constructed farm-to-market road and to the traversing public.

We recommended that the MTO furnish the MACCO with the duly certified list of Real Property Tax Receivables/Special Education Tax Receivables based on the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year and not by barangay only as its basis in the recording.
We recommended that the MACCO reduce the amount of the recorded receivables and deferred income by P1,957,393.00, by drawing the appropriate entry adjusting the balances of the affected accounts.
We also recommended that the MACCO make representation with the ECPAC Systems Corporation for a possible solution in the recording of subsidiary records for a large number of taxpayers. Look into the possibility of converting the list of taxpayers provided by the ETRACS to be conveniently fed into the system.
We recommended that the MACCO coordinate with the MTO on its latest report of delinquent properties, excluding those RPUs under Republic of the Philippines, and prepare the necessary entries to correctly set the receivables and deferred income accounts.
We recommended that the municipal accountant review the entries made pertaining to RPT/SET advance payments. Prepare reconciliation of the abstract of collections as received from the MTO and as generated from the ECPAC to establish the correct amount of advance collections to be adjusted. Furnish the audit team with copies of the JEV.

We recommended that the Municipality stop charging fuel and other related expenses against the LDRRMF unless only for disaster risk management activities as provided under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and other related issuances to ensure that the objectives set forth in the allocation of the LDRRMF are achieved thru proper usage thereof.
We recommended that the MDRRMO follow and adhere to the submission of LDRRMFIP in accordance with COA Circular 2012-002, which includes the presentation of all necessary data, including the status and prioritization of the prior years’ continuing appropriations to ensure detailed planning.

We recommended that the MGSO request authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to authorize the Local Chief Executive to transfer the completed projects of prior years to the respective beneficiaries and prepare the Invoice Receipts (IR) Gen. Form No. 30-A to support the Certificate of Turn-over and Acceptance of Completed Projects to be received by the recipient schools and barangays. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the Journal Entry Voucher/s (JEV/s) for the dropping of the affected PPE accounts from the books with the following attachments: (1) Authority from SB; (2) JEV of the beneficiaries taking up the PPEs received by them; (3) Invoice Receipt (IR) and (4) Certificate of Turn-over and Acceptance of Completed Projects duly received by the recipients.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the KCAST President and Municipal Accountant to exert effort to locate the supporting documents to prove the validity of the claims and to enforce payment thereof.  If found to be no longer payable or without valid claimants which are two-year-old or over and/or other undocumented accounts payable irrespective of age, it should be reverted to unappropriated surplus and funds can be used to finance various projects and programs. 

We recommended that the MACCO record the RPT shares of the Province and Barangays under Due to LGU account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to book the correct amount of the acquisition cost of the land (P7,309,200.00) and the corresponding unrecorded payable to Mr. Daniel A. Manuat, Jr. in the amount of P2,260,700.00.

We recommended that:

The MACCO and the MEO reconcile their records to determine the actual status of the said projects;

The MEO provide the MACCO with copies of the report of completed projects and other necessary documents to serve as the basis for the transfer of these projects from CIP accounts to the appropriate PPE accounts; and

Based on the adjusted data, the MACCO computes the required depreciation on the completed projects, and accordingly adjust the balances of the affected accounts.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all AOs (ICO- Municipal
Treasurer, collectors, disbursing officers and special disbursing officers) to strictly
comply with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002. Glaring violation/s of the COA
guidelines on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances will result in possible
suspension and/or disallowance in the audit of the transactions involved.
Further, all AOs should regularly update, balance and reconcile their cashbooks
with the records of the accounting office to ensure that the recorded transactions are
complete, reliable, accurate and timely.
The collectors must also ensure that an accountable form is properly filled up
before issuing the same.

systems by requiring:

Agriculturist to be matched/reconciled regularly with the ledger cards of the
accounting unit and discrepancy(ies), if any, shall be immediately verified and
adjusted;

mortalities of dispersal animals, update their records and copy furnish the
accounting department to facilitate reconciliation and reflect the correct
balance of the account Breeding Stocks;

substantiate all claims of loss through death of said animals with complete
documentary attachments upon filing of a request for relief from
accountability for the proper disposition thereof pursuant to Section 151 of
COA Circular No. 92-386 to the audit team; and
4) The concerned officials and employees conduct regular inspection of stocks
dispersed and physical inventory of existing animals in accordance with
existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations. Periodic reconciliation

collection of the P1,605,240.00 Loans Receivable-Others from its borrowers and to
update their records to include any loan repayment scheme that may subsequently be
agreed upon the municipality and its borrowers and furnish reports to the Accounting
Office on a monthly basis for reconciliation and monitoring purposes.
We also recommended that management should undertake reasonable efforts to
collect these debts owing from the borrowers' lest the intention of the program benefits
will be defeated. Likewise, submit a request for write-off of the loan balances of deceased
borrowers substantiated by valid supporting documents.
We further recommended that Management thoroughly review the grant of
Allowance for Impairment Loss without adequate and relevant documentation to avoid
loss of assets.

Recommendations:

Receivables in order to establish a realistic collection target of real property taxes
at the start of the year; and
2. Observe the proper recording of entries as prescribed under the NGAS Manual so
that reported accounts are reflected fairly and accurately in the financial
statements of the Municipality.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
exhaust all available remedies provided under the law, rules and regulations in enforcing
collection of the amount of disallowance/charges and strictly observe the provisions of
Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-06. Further, maintain complete records of audit
disallowances/charges for regular and effective monitoring.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the responsible officials and employees of the
Municipality observe prudence in the discharge of their duties so as government funds
are appropriately safeguarded through the following measures:
1. In case a Letter of Credit is issued by the Municipality, the BAC should ensure
that the requirements of Section 42.6 of the Revised IRR of the R.A. No. 9184 are
fully observed and if the winning bidder is a local supplier, the LC should be
issued to the latter and not to its foreign manufacturer or supplier;
2. No payment shall be made on a Letter of Credit unless the goods have been
delivered and accepted as certified by the Municipality in accordance with the
delivery schedule provided for in the contract;
3. The Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office submit a
written explanation on the non-imposition of liquidated damages on the delays
committed by the supplier and collect the same from the supplier;
4. The Municipal Treasurer’s Office should submit the following lacking
documents:
• For imported items:
Home Consumption Value of the items
o
Breakdown of the expenses incurred in the Importation
o
• Original copy of Dealer’s/Supplier’s Invoices showing the quantity,
description of the articles, unit and total value, duly signed by the dealer or his
representative, and indicating receipt by the proper agency official of items
delivered.
• Tax receipts from the Bureau of Customs or the BIR indicating the exact
specifications and/or serial number of the equipment procured by the
government as proof of payment of all taxes and duties due on the same
75
equipment, supplied or sold to the government [Administrative Order (AO)
No. 200 dated November 21, 1990]
Henceforth, payments made to suppliers should be supported with the necessary
documentary requirements particularly the sales invoice.
5. The Municipal Accounting Office should require the supplier, ConEquip
Philippines Inc. to provide the Municipality the necessary warranty security on
the equipment procured, either in the form of cash or a special bank guarantee
equivalent to at least 1% of the total contract price of P13,853,000.00;
6. The Municipal Accounting Office should require the supplier, ConEquip
Philippines Inc. to pay the necessary withholding taxes amounting to P742,125.00
or any evidence establishing the payment of taxes on the equipment procured.

observe prudence in the discharge of their duties so as government funds are
appropriately safeguarded such as through the following measures:

Recommendations:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Treasurer and
other officials concerned to undertake effective, efficient, and aggressive tax collection
strategies to improve collection.
For the delinquent real property taxes, we recommend the management to impose
the legal remedies either by administrative auction thru levy on real property or judicial
auction to enforce collection and to undertake the same expeditiously on a yearly basis to
reduce amounts accumulated as receivables for delinquent taxes.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality strictly utilize the 5% Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) only for disaster risk management
activities as provided under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 so as to ensure that
the objectives set forth in the allocation of the LDRRMF are achieved thru proper usage
thereof.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting
entries to transfer the unexpended balance of the Trust Liability – DRRM in reverting the
CY 2013 balance to the General Fund and be made available for other social services as
identified by its local sanggunian. Henceforth, the accounting office should observe the
five-year rule period in the utilization of DRRM fund.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal Solid
Waste Management Board should ensure efficiency and effectiveness on the
implementation of the solid waste management disposal program so that all garbage is
properly handled and disposed of in the Recovery Containment Area instead of just being
dumped which is detrimental to the environment and health of the locality. Moreover,
install perimeter barriers or fence to prohibit unauthorized access to the site.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality fully observe existing laws, rules and
regulations pertaining to the hiring of job order personnel. We further recommend that
contracts/memorandum of agreement/job orders be executed between the Municipality
and individuals hired as COS and JOs in accordance with these laws to define the rights
and obligations of the contracting parties and avoid possible suspensions in the audit of
affected transactions.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to examine all
documents submitted by the suppliers/bidders which includes checking its contents and
validity in compliance with the procurement law.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the Head of Procuring
Entity require the Municipal Engineer and Accountant to deduct/collect the correct
amount of liquidated damages from the contractor.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the Local Chief Executive carry
out the following:
1. Exercise proper planning in the identification of beneficiaries and places for
which any construction of the PWS would be built to ensure that there is an
existing potable and sufficient water source;
2. Exercise prudence in supervising the execution of the contract in order that any
delay and inefficiency in the construction of the potable water system would be
prevented;
3. Ensure that after completion, the project is functional and usable for the purpose it
was intended.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the Local Chief Executive,
conduct an immediate investigation to determine the persons responsible who wrongfully
caused the destruction of the post-harvest facility without authority and compel the
payment of actual damages incurred therefrom. Undertake legal steps, if necessary.
We also recommended that LGU Kapalong regularly monitor all completed
projects, regardless of fund sources to evaluate if the projects achieved the desired
purpose for the benefit of intended recipients.
Further, we recommended that the LCE require concerned officials to fully
observe existing auditing guidelines on the disposal of government property to avoid
wastage.

Recommendations:
We recommended that:
1. The Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal
Engineer to fast track the implementation/completion of projects from prior and
current years that are past beyond their target completion date so that government
funds do not remain idle and unutilized for several years. Regularly monitor the
status and utilization of the development fund detailing the timeframe of each
project; and
2. The Municipality should also conduct a re-evaluation as to whether the existing
unutilized programs are still its priority concerns taking into consideration current
developments and to accordingly re-appropriate the funds of projects/programs
which have become irrelevant or unresponsive to current priority undertakings to
ensure that priority projects are carried out in a timely manner and its purpose be
achieved to the benefit of the people.

Recommendations:
To ensure that the resources are managed in the most effective and efficient
manner to meet the needs of the constituents and stakeholders of the municipality, we
recommended the following:
1. Carefully plan and organize the project management plan. Often, project planning is
ignored in favor of getting on with the work;
2. Fast track the implementation of unimplemented PPAs;
3. Evaluate whether the programs and projects funded from prior year’s budget are still
relevant, otherwise, revert the fund to other programs and projects which are more
needed and applicable upon recommendation of Local Chief Executive and approval
by the Sanggunian (Section 322 of RA No. 7160);
4. The Municipal Development Council (MDC) should strictly monitor and evaluate
PPAs (Section 112 of RA 7160) and provide project support or program
maintenance/monitoring.
5. Strictly abide by the provisions of Section 4, Annex A of IRR of RA 9184. Feasibility
study and detailed engineering should be mandatory before implementing any project.

Recommendations: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Inspectorate Committee to ensure that all received and inspected deliveries conform to the specification of POs, any deviations thereto should be justified in writing.
We also recommended that for the similar future projects, for transparency, the beneficiaries must be informed of all the goods expected to be received as well as be invited in all procurement stage.
Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the offices/committees concerned to submit justification letter with appropriate supporting documents to explain the above noted deficiencies, otherwise, the said disbursements shall be suspended/disallowed in audit.

Recommendation: We recommended that the officials and employees concerned adhere strictly with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No.2017-1 on appropriating and utilizing the 20% DF for development projects.

Recommendation:  We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Accounting Office to record all purchases in an appropriate inventory accounts and report issuances based on submitted SSMI by the GSO. Prepare likewise the appropriate JEV to adjust the erroneous entry made.

Recommendation:  We recommended that the LDRRMC headed by the Municipal Mayor adhere strictly to the guidelines set under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated 25 March 2013 on the allocation and utilization of the LDRRMF. Stop the practice of charging expenses which are not related and do not support disaster risk reduction management.

Recommendation:  We recommended that the LDRRMO ensure that the RDS be properly filled-up and a separate record to monitor the goods by kind should be prepared to serve as a guide in determining actual inventory levels. Likewise, install appropriate measures in safeguarding its inventories to avoid the risks of inventory infestation, pilferage, spoilage and expiration of goods.

Recommendation/s: We recommended that an annual transfer of excess collections over payments made for the honoraria and overtime pay of the members of the BAC should be made to be recorded as income in the General Fund.

Recommendation: We recommended that the MACCO requires the submission of an accomplishment report as supporting documents to the payrolls in compliance to Circular 2012-001.  Also, it is advised that payrolls should be prepared by the Admin or Human Resource Management Office.

Recommendations: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters requiring the persons liable to settle their received NDs within the prescribed period.
We also recommended that the MACCO submit the individual subsidiary ledgers of the person’s liable to aid in the reconciliation of the said account.

Recommendation:  We recommended that Management require its concerned offices to ensure that the required and appropriate withholding of taxes to all payments made to its Officers and Employees be made in accordance to tax laws, otherwise, the penalty provision of the said Order might be imposed.

Recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Person and members of the council undergo relevant GAD trainings to capacitate them on the preparation of GAD-PB, GAD-AR and in the administration of the HGDG test. Moreover, ensure that all GAD attributed PPAs is administered through HGDG.

Recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned offices to ensure that all supporting documents are properly filled up and contain only factual information relative thereto.

Recommendation/s: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned offices to ensure that all changes in the mode of procurement should be reflected in the APP and submitted to the GPPB in accordance with Section 7.4 of the IRR of R.A. 9184. Moreover, the mandatory review and evaluation of the terms, conditions, and specifications in the bidding documents must be conducted to justify the adoption of the alternative mode of procurement.

Recommendation: We recommended that management should maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF from current year’s appropriation and prior years’ unexpended balances to enable the Municipality to implement projects related to disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness within the scheduled timeframe.

Submit the certified list of taxpayers supporting the amounts reflected as RPT and SET Receivables as of December 31, 2014.

observations.recommendation:
The policy for the operation of the Heavy Equipment Rental should be revisited. See to it that the objectives for which this economic enterprise was ventured shall be attained.  Utilization of the heavy equipment by clientele who cannot afford to pay rental as in the case of barangay, may be recognized with a corresponding subsidy account to be incorporated in the financial plan of the economic enterprise. 

Otherwise, the Municipality may cease the operation of the Heavy Equipment Rental as economic enterprise and turn it over as a regular operation of the municipality under the general fund proper.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant recognize in the books the  valid
book reconciling items noted in the Bank  Reconciliation  Statements  as  of
December 31, 2016 and reconcile further the year-end balances  to  determine
the noted variance of P19,930.51.

During the exit conference conducted on March  9,  2017,  the  OIC-Municipal
Accountant committed to record in the books the book reconciling  items  and
will look into the noted variance to completely reconcile the cash  in  bank
book and bank records.

Recommendation:

We recommend that the Municipality Mayor direct  the  responsible  officials
of the Municipality to:


   1. Complete the annual physical inventory  of  the  property,  plant  and
      equipment;
   2. Prepare the Report on  the  Physical  Count  of  Property,  Plant  and
      Equipment (RPCPPE);
   3. Conduct reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the accounting records; and
   4. Submit the RPCPPE to COA not later than January 31 of each year.


In the exit conference, management  committed  to  implement  the  foregoing
audit recommendation in CY 2017.

Recommendation:

We recommend that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  Municipal  Accounting
Office to effect the necessary  adjusting  entry  taking  up  the  completed
projects under their appropriate Buildings and Other Structures accounts.


During the exit conference, the OIC-Municipal Accountant signified  to  make
the necessary adjusting entries to properly classify the  afore-cited  items
of Property, Plant and Equipment.

Recommendation:

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal  Accountant
to  provide  depreciation  on  all  the  depreciable  Property,  Plant   and
Equipment of the Municipality in accordance with the existing regulations.


The OIC-Municipal Accountant  committed,  during  the  exit  conference,  to
provide depreciation on all the depreciable Property,  Plant  and  Equipment
of the Municipality.

Recommendation:

We recommend that  the  Municipal  Mayor  should  instruct  the  responsible
official(s) of the Municipality to select Civil Society  Organizations  duly
accredited by the DSWD as beneficiary of government programs/projects.

In his letter dated March 6, 2017,  the  Municipal  Agriculturist  explained
that prior to the selection of the  BuB  project  beneficiaries,  the  LGU’s
Local Poverty Reduction Action Team (LPRAT) conducted consultation with  the
CSOs’ representatives and projects were  identified  based  on  the  program
menu of the Department of Agriculture. Further, all the requirements  needed
were carefully examined and complied accordingly by the CSOs.

He continued to state, however, that COA-DBM-DSWD JR No.  2014-001  was  not
part  of  the  requirements  given,  thus  its  set  of  criteria  was   not
considered.  Nevertheless,  in  the  coming   national   projects   of   the
Municipality, COA-DBM-DSWD JR No. 2014-001 will be strictly abided.

Recommendation:

We  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  should  direct  the  responsible
official(s) of the Municipality  to  submit  the  Memorandum  of  Agreements
(MOAs)  pertaining  to  all  BuB  and  other  NGA-funded  projects  of   the
Municipality for the FY 2015 and 2016.

Henceforth, all  MOAs  covering  fund  transfers  from  national  government
agencies should be  submitted  to  the  Audit  Team  within  the  prescribed
period.

During the exit conference, management informed  the  Audit  Team  that  the
Municipality submitted the MOAs  to  the  regional  offices  of  the  source
agencies but it took some time before the source agencies returned the  same
to the Municipality; hence the MOAs were not submitted immediately.

COA’s Rejoinder:

The Municipality should submit  copies  of  MOAs  duly  received  by  source
agencies  for  signing  purposes  to  the  Audit  Team   and   just   submit
subsequently the duly signed and notarized copies.

Recommendation:

We  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  should  direct  the  responsible
official(s) of the Municipality to follow the requirements of  COA  Circular
No. 2013-004 on the appropriate tarpaulin signboards  to  be  installed  for
infrastructure projects and likewise, instruct the creation and  maintenance
of its own website so as to fulfill the transparency requirement of the BuB
Program and the procurement law, in general.
In his letter dated March 7, 2017, the Municipal  Engineer  stated  that  on
succeeding projects  of  the  Municipality,  tarpaulin  signboard  specified
under COA Circular No. 2013-004 will be provided.

On the other hand, the Municipal  Information  Officer  informed  the  Audit
Team, in his letter dated March 6, 2017, that the LGU  has  no  website  for
almost 2 years as the previous website with  the  domain  lgukapalong.gov.ph
was deactivated.  As  of  the  moment,  however,  the  LGU  has  a  new  and
functional domain: kapalong.gov.ph.

Recommendation:

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the  Supply  Office  and  the
Bids and Awards Committee submit to  the  Audit  Team  the  lacking  bidding
documents  on  the  identified   procurement   projects.   Henceforth,   all
procurement  contracts  and/or  purchase  orders  should  be  supported   by
applicable  documents  forming  part  thereof  as  provided  for  under  COA
Circular No. 2009-001.

On March 7, 2017, the BAC Chairman submitted the lacking  bidding  documents
on the above-mentioned procurement projects.

Recommendation:

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should direct:

   1. The  Office  of  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to  keep  custody  of  all
      inventoriable supplies after delivery and inspection of  the  same  by
      the inspection committee;

   2. The Municipal Accounting Office to record all  inventoriable  supplies
      under their appropriate inventory accounts upon purchase based on  the
      Acceptance and Inspection Report and other supporting documents;

   3. The Municipal Agriculture Office to submit  the  pertinent  withdrawal
      and/or acknowledgment receipts  supporting  the  distribution  of  the
      agricultural supplies to the members of the beneficiary civil  society
      organizations.


The Municipal Accounting Office submitted on  March  8,  the  journal  entry
voucher (JEV) No. 300-2017-02-00118 dated February 27,  2017  taking  up  in
the books the afore-cited  supplies  under  the  account  “Agricultural  and
Marine Supplies for Distribution”.


On the same date, the Municipal Agriculture Office submitted  the  pertinent
acknowledgment receipts for equipment/supplies supporting  the  distribution
of the agricultural  supplies  to  the  members  of  the  beneficiary  civil
society organizations.

Recommendation:

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should direct:

   1.  The  Municipal  Accounting  Office  to  record  all  assets  procured
      pertaining to projects funded by national government agencies  and  to
      be implemented by beneficiary civil society organizations (CSOs); and

   2. The Municipal Agriculture Office to submit the pertinent memorandum of
      agreements entered into between the Municipality and beneficiary  CSOs
      supporting the projects funded by national government agencies and  to
      be implemented with the CSOs.

On March 8, 2017, the Municipal Accounting  Office  submitted  the  copy  of
journal entry voucher (JEV) No. 100-2017-02-00890 dated  February  22,  2017
recognizing the afore-cited assets under their respective accounts.

On the same date, the Municipal Agriculture Office submitted  the  pertinent
acknowledgment receipts for equipment supporting the receipt of  the  afore-
cited assets by the beneficiaries.

Recommendation:

We  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  should  instruct  the  Municipal
Accounting Office to require the concerned NGOs/POs to  liquidate  the  long
outstanding funds transferred to them; failure to liquidate the same  should
disqualify the  said  NGOs/POs  from  availing  additional  funds  from  the
Municipality.

During the exit conference, the OIC-Municipal Accountant informed the  Audit
Team that they have communicated  already  the  matter  with  the  concerned
NGOs/POs. As of  March  8,  2017,  it  was  known  that  Luna  Multi-Purpose
Cooperative (MPC) was already dissolved since 2002 and some of the  officers
and members were deceased as stated in the letter dated  March  8,  2017  of
its chairman. Hence, they could no longer settle their obligation  with  the
LGU.

In this regard, management is looking into  the  possibility  of  requesting
for the write-off of the said receivable.

In the  case  of  the  Gabuyan  Irrigators  Multi-Purpose  Cooperative,  the
Municipality is still awaiting its reply.

COA’s Rejoinder:

Management is enjoined to follow-up the settlement of the  said  receivables
and if warranted by circumstances,  should  request  for  the  write-of  the
same.

Recommendation:

We  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  should  direct   the   Municipal
Accounting Office to record  the  funds  transferred  to  various  barangays
amounting to P9,232,956.61 under the  account  “Due  from  Local  Government
Units” using the following journal entry and require the liquidation of  the
same:

Due from Local Government Units              P9,232,956.61
   Due from Non-Government Organizations/
       People’s Organizations                                  P1,571,198.11
   Due to NGAs                                       7,661,758.50

The Municipal Accounting Office submitted on  March  8,  2017  the  copy  of
journal entry voucher (JEV) No. 100-2017-02-00110 dated  February  27,  2017
taking up the foregoing journal entry.

Recommendation:

We  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  should  direct   the   Municipal
Accounting Office to submit the Memorandums of Agreement pertaining  to  the
funds transferred amounting to P3,630,000.00 to  various  barangays  out  of
the KALAHI-CIDSS Trust  Fund.  Henceforth,  all  fund  transfers  should  be
supported by the necessary documentary  requirements  prescribed  under  COA
Circular No. 2012-001.


On March 8, 2017, the Municipal Accounting Office  submitted  the  following
documents:

    • Copy of MOA as authorized by Municipal Resolution No.  14,  series  of
      2013;
    • Copy of Program of Works for KC-PAMANA;
    • Copy of Program of Works for KC-NCDDP; and
    • Copy of official receipts issued by the barangays to the LGU.

Recommendation:

We  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  should  direct   the   Municipal
Accounting Office to transfer the unexpended balance of the  Quick  Response
Fund and DRRMF-MOOE to the special trust fund  solely  for  the  purpose  of
supporting  disaster  risk  reduction  and  management  activities  of   the
Municipality within the next five (5) years.


On March 6, 2017, the OIC-Municipal Accountant informed the Audit Team  thru
a letter that the amount was  already  transferred  to  the  trust  fund  as
evidenced  by  journal  entry  voucher  (JEV)  No.  100-2017-02-00375  dated
February 2, 2017. Likewise, her office committed to strictly adhere  to  COA
Circular No. 2012-002 for the transfer of the unexpended balance of the  QRF
and the DRRMF-MOOE at the end CY 2017 and beyond.

Recommendation:

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of  the  Municipal  Solid
Waste  Management  Board,  direct   the   responsible   officials   of   the
Municipality  to  incorporate  in  its  10-Year   Ecological   Solid   Waste
Management Plan necessary capital expenditures, such as but not  limited  to
the provision of a sanitary landfill and adequate garbage trucks, so  as  to
fully implement the provisions and purposes of the Act.


In the exit conference,  the  Municipal  Mayor  signified  that  appropriate
measures  have  been  adopted  to  answer  the  noted  deficiencies  in  the
implementation of the solid waste management program of the Municipality.

Recommendation:

We recommend that the Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal  Accounting
Office to establish the Municipal Solid Waste Management Fund as  a  special
account in the General Fund and accordingly, prepare the required  financial
reports on the Fund.

During the exit conference, the Municipal  Accounting  Office  committed  to
establish and maintain a special account in the general fund for  the  Solid
Waste Management Fund.

Recommendation:

We  recommend  that  the  Local  School   Board   carefully   evaluate   the
programs/projects/activities to be included in the SEF  Budget  taking  into
consideration the specific PPAs set forth under Section 1 of  R.A  No.  5447
and Sections 100 (c) 272 of  R.A.  No.  7160.  Otherwise,  expenditures  not
falling within those allowed under the said  laws  shall  be  disallowed  in
audit.

In a letter dated March 3, 2017, the  district  supervisors  of  the  DepEd-
Kapalong East  and  West  Districts  commented  that  the  services  of  the
Municipal Literacy Program staff and drivers were necessary because  of  the
opening of 15 IP schools in the Municipality  in  2016;  nevertheless  their
wages will not be included under the 2017 SEF Budget.

On the other hand, the school supplies purchased were intended  for  the  IP
children in the IP schools of Barangay Gupitan  which  have  benefited  them
accordingly; nevertheless, the findings and suggestions of  the  Audit  Team
will be followed in the succeeding allocations.

Recommendation:

We  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  should  instruct  the  Municipal
Accounting Office to submit immediately to the Audit  Team  the  Reports  of
Checks Issued with Disbursement Voucher  and  Liquidation  Reports  for  the
month of December 2016.

Henceforth, the Municipal Accounting Office should observe  the  submission,
within the prescribed period, of the Report  of  Collections  and  Deposits,
Disbursement Vouchers, Liquidation Reports together with the  Journal  Entry
Vouchers and general journal transactions to the Audit Team.

In her letter dated March 6, 2017,  the  OIC-Municipal  Accountant  informed
the Audit Team the Reports of Checks Issued with  Disbursement  Voucher  and
Liquidation Reports for the month of December 2016 was already submitted.

Further, she justified that the incompatibility  of  the  ECPAC  (accounting
system) and the ETRACS (used by the Municipal Treasurer’s  Office)  was  one
of the factors that  contributed  in  the  delay  of  generating  Collection
Reports especially for the Real Property Tax collections; the in-charge  has
to encode the collections in both systems before the reports  are  generated
and submitted to the Accounting Office.

On the other hand, the Reports of Checks Issued with  Disbursement  Vouchers
for the months of October to December 2016 were  not  submitted  within  the
prescribed period due to the leave of absence of  the  accounting  in-charge
by reason of health problem. Presently, two personnel were assigned  in  the
segregation of DVs’ supporting documents, however, the 10 days  deadline  is
not enough with all the encoding of the same  to  the  BLGF  SRE  and  other
treasury entries before the DVs will be forwarded to the Accounting  Office.
On the Liquidation Reports, corrective measures were  already  set  up  with
the disbursing officer.
She continued to state that the delays in  the  submission  of  the  monthly
financial  reports  will  be  addressed  if  not  minimized  with  the   new
management of the  Accounting  Office.  Inter-office  coordination  will  be
observed. However, the 10 days required submission deadline  is  deemed  not
enough  for  both  the  Accounting  Office  and  the   Treasurer’s   Office.
Accordingly, the OIC-Municipal Accountant  requested  for  an  extension  of
another 10 days in addition to the prescribed period set by the Commission.

COA’s Rejoinder:

In  view  of  the  foregoing,  management  is  reminded  that,  unless   the
prescribed period of submitting the monthly reports  within  10  days  after
the end of the month is amended by the  Commission,  it  has  to  abide  and
submit its monthly financial reports within the deadline set.

Recommendation:

We  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  should  direct   the   Municipal
Accounting  Office  to  submit  the  year-end   financial   statements   and
supporting schedules of the Municipality on or before February  14  of each
year to the Audit Team.

Likewise,  submit  the  monthly  trial  balance  and   quarterly   financial
statements on or before the prescribed period to the Audit Team.


The OIC-Municipal Accountant, in her letter dated March 6,  2017,  explained
that their accounting system software encountered problems  for  almost  two
weeks causing delay in the closing of books of the  LGU;  they  have  sought
the assistance of its developer in Davao City several  times  just  to  find
solution to said dilemma. However, the same  was  resolved  only  after  the
February 14, 2017 deadline.

The  OIC-Municipal  Accountant  assured  the  Audit  Team  that   succeeding
financial reports including the year-end financial  statements  as  well  as
the monthly  trial  balance  and  quarterly  financial  statements  will  be
submitted within the prescribed period.

Recommendation:

We recommend that management strictly follow  the  requirements  of  RA  No.
7160 or the Local Government Code as  to  the  preparation  and  sources  of
funds of its supplemental budgets.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize in the books the valid book reconciling items noted in the Bank Reconciliation Statements as of December 31, 2015 and reconcile further the year-end balances to determine the noted variance of P61,856.50.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the balances of the two reciprocal accounts and make the necessary adjustments thereto, to fully eliminate the difference in the accounts.

observations.reason: Physical count was already conducted but not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the specific items of Property, Plant and Equipment included in the schedules supporting the balances of PPE presented in the year-end financial statements under their appropriate classifications based on the descriptions provided for under the Revised Chart of Accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant transfer the unexpended balance of the Quick Response Fund and Mitigation Funds-MOOE to the special trust fund solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Municipality within the next five (5) years.

We recommended that the Municipality submit at the beginning of the year to the Supervising Auditor and Audit Team Leader the list of all ongoing projects/programs/activities including those non-infrastructure PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year.

Likewise, it was recommended that the implementing offices of the Municipality inform its Supervising Auditor and Audit Team Leader within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.

Furthermore, it was recommended that the Municipality submit the Quarterly Report on Publicized Projects/Programs/Activities on or before the 10th day of the succeeding quarter to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The imple-menting offices of the LGU still failed to inform the SA and the ATL within ten (10) days after the award of the infra project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.

observations.recommendation: It was recommended that the Municipality stop the awarding of procurement contracts and/or purchase orders to the Kapalong Government Officials and Employees Cooperative to which Management agreed to abide by the audit recommendation.

We recommend that the Municipality take the following measures:

Stop the payment of honorarium to the concerned personnel.

Appoint a new College Administrator/President who could render full time service in accordance with Section 19 of CHED CMO No. 04, Series of 2007.
observations.reason: Full-time College President was not hired.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipality take the following courses of action:
	
Conduct physical inventory of the property, plant and equipment and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).

Prepare reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the Accounting records and submit the same to COA. 

Prepare a separate report on unserviceable and obsolete, including those assets which, though still serviceable, are no longer used in operation using the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP).

Direct the Accounting Office to reclassify the assets listed in the IIRUP to the Other Assets account.

Require the Disposal Committee to plan the disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the guidelines set under Section 79 of PD 1445, including requesting for assistance from COA technical inspectors.

Direct the inventory committee to complete the conduct of physical count of all property of the municipality and determine the asset’s condition as to existence, serviceability or un serviceability;

Provide depreciation for all depreciable items using the estimated useful life provided under COA Circular 2003-007; and

For land acquired by the agency, ensure that all real estate are registered, under the Torrens title System and safeguarded from the squatters or other unlawful occupants.
observations.reason: Physical count was made but not yet completed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive take the following courses of action:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books of accounts the loans and interests receivables as of November 30, 2014 and create subsidiary ledgers of the concerned borrowers to that effect.

Instruct the PESO Manager to keep the loan records of the borrowers in a ledger-type document so as to easily track the balances and submit reports to the Accounting Office on a monthly basis for reconciliation and monitoring purposes.
Instruct the PESO Manager to request for write-off of the loan balances of deceased borrowers supported by the related death certificates and forward the same to COA for proper disposition.
observations.reason:
Loans and interest receivables were not completely recognized in the books; P237,980.00  repayments are still recorded under Other Payables account;
the PESO still does not maintain ledger-type records to account the loans and submit monthly reports to the Accounting Office; and loans of deceased debtors still exist.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management immediately use the amount deposited in the HYSA to pay off portion of the principal amount of the loan and to provide a written explanation why the unrestricted fund placed on time deposit was not utilized.
observations.reason: The HYSA account was transferred to the current account and declared as savings for supple-mental budget purposes.
